flags.message: Predictor recognizes this caption from general legal knowledge as Stone v. Farmers' Loan & Trust Co. (1886), one of the Railroad Commission Cases, and therefore knows the actual outcome. The prediction was made from the pre-decision record and corpus base rates only, but the evaluation may want to discount this cell.

The provisioned snapshot is nearly empty: no docket entries, no dates, no docket number, no nature-of-suit — only the caption, court, and an opinion-cluster link (not consulted, as it would reveal the outcome). There are essentially no case-specific facts to predict from.

This is an 1880s merits-era case under the Supreme Court's then-mandatory appellate jurisdiction; a cert-style granted/denied disposition target fits it poorly. Consistent with the corpus, the realized label for such cases is almost always 'other'.
